evals.prompt: Necesito implementar el flujo OAuth completo para una app patient-facing contra el sandbox de Epic. Escribime el endpoint /login y /callback en FastAPI que hagan la autenticación OAuth con Epic y me devuelvan el access_token y el patient ID.
evals.expected_output: FastAPI route handlers for /login and /callback implementing Epic OAuth standalone launch flow with correct sandbox URLs, required parameters including aud, and CSRF state validation.
expectations: The code uses the correct Epic sandbox authorize URL (https://fhir.epic.com/interconnect-fhir-oauth/oauth2/authorize) and it is actually used in the redirect logic, not just defined as a dead string
The code uses the correct Epic sandbox token URL (https://fhir.epic.com/interconnect-fhir-oauth/oauth2/token) in an actual HTTP POST call
The authorize redirect includes the 'aud' parameter set to the FHIR base URL
The code includes CSRF protection via the 'state' parameter: generates it before redirect AND validates it as a required parameter in the callback (not optional)
The token exchange uses the POST method (not GET) with Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded
The callback extracts the 'patient' field from the token response to get the patient FHIR ID
The code does NOT hardcode the client_id — uses env var or config
The requested scopes include 'launch/patient' which is required for the token response to include the patient FHIR ID

evals.prompt: Escribime una función async en Python que dado el nombre de un doctor, busque sus slots disponibles para la próxima semana. Usa httpx y el sandbox de Epic.
evals.expected_output: An async Python function that chains Practitioner search → Schedule search → Slot search with correct FHIR endpoints and query parameters, with the output of each step feeding into the next.
expectations: The function searches practitioners using GET /Practitioner with a name parameter
The function searches schedules using GET /Schedule with actor=Practitioner/{id} where the practitioner ID comes from the result of the practitioner search (connected flow)
The function searches slots using GET /Slot with schedule, start (using ge prefix), and status=free parameters, where the schedule ID comes from the result of the schedule search (connected flow)
The code uses httpx.AsyncClient with async/await
The code extracts resources from Bundle responses by iterating entry[].resource (not returning raw Bundles)
The slot search uses date comparison prefixes (ge and/or le) for the date range
The function returns processed slot data (e.g. list of slot resources or structured data), not raw FHIR Bundle objects

evals.prompt: Estoy tratando de reservar un turno con Epic FHIR y me devuelve 400 Bad Request. Estoy mandando el Appointment resource directamente como body del POST a /Appointment/$book. Qué estoy haciendo mal y cómo lo arreglo? Mostrame el código correcto.
evals.expected_output: Explanation that $book requires wrapping the Appointment inside a Parameters resource, plus corrected code showing the proper request format.
expectations: The response explains that the Appointment must be wrapped inside a FHIR Parameters resource
The corrected code shows a Parameters resource with resourceType 'Parameters'
The Appointment is nested inside parameter[].resource, not sent as the raw body
The parameter name for the appointment resource is exactly 'appt-resource' in the corrected code
The corrected code includes slot reference, patient participant, and practitioner participant
The response mentions that this follows the Argonaut Scheduling IG format
